answer: In 2024, global investment in solar PV reached a record USD 554 billion, a 49% increase, surpassing fossil fuel investments. Renewable energy capacity grew by 15.1%, reaching 4,448 GW, but still falls short of climate goals. Renewables provided 32% of global electricity in 2024.

results.title: Renewable Energy Set a New Record in 2024, But It's Still Not ...
results.snippet: Global renewable energy capacity reached a record 4,448 GW in 2024, yet it still falls short of the 11.2 terawatts needed to meet climate goals.

results.title: Renewables provided record 32% of global electricity in 2024 ...
results.snippet: The growth of renewable power generation - including wind, hydro and solar - in the global electricity mix in 2024 beat the previous year's 30%

results.title: Renewable energy capacity surged around the world in 2024
results.snippet: Global renewable energy capacity grew by 15.1% in 2024, largely driven by solar. Yet a growth rate of at least 16.6% must be maintained to reach

results.title: Executive summary – Renewables 2024 – Analysis - IEA
results.snippet: Global renewable capacity is expected to grow by 2.7 times by 2030, surpassing countries' current ambitions by nearly 25%, but it still falls short of tripling.
